completion_promises.criterion_text: build_pipeline_consumer_deps(client, forge_config, sqlite_pool) returns a PipelineConsumerDeps with all four fields wired: forge_config, is_duplicate_terminal, dispatch_build, publish_build_failed.
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented build_pipeline_consumer_deps in src/forge/cli/_serve_deps.py. The factory composes the three Wave-2 collaborator factories (forward_context_builder, stage_log_recorder, state_channel) plus the publisher/emitter pair, and wires four field closures: (a) forge_config passed through; (b) _build_is_duplicate_terminal returns an async closure that issues SELECT status against the (feature_id, correlation_id) unique index per ASSUM-014; (c) _build_dispatch_build calls dispatch_autobuild_async with the four wave-2 collaborators + lifecycle_emitter; (d) _build_publish_build_failed wraps PipelinePublisher.publish_build_failed. Verified by tests/cli/test_serve_deps.py::TestFactoryWiresAllFourFields (4 tests).

completion_promises.criterion_text: make_handle_message_dispatcher(deps) returns a closure conforming to (_MsgLike) -> Awaitable[None].
completion_promises.evidence: Implemented in src/forge/cli/_serve_dispatcher.py: factory returns an async def dispatch(msg: _MsgLike) -> None closure that satisfies the DispatchFn type alias from forge.cli._serve_daemon. Verified by tests/cli/test_serve_dispatcher.py::TestDispatcherClosureSignature (2 tests assert inspect.iscoroutinefunction and single-positional-argument signature).

completion_promises.criterion_text: The closure delegates to pipeline_consumer.handle_message(msg, deps) and does not call msg.ack() itself.
completion_promises.evidence: The dispatcher body is a single line: `await pipeline_consumer.handle_message(msg, deps)`. handle_message is intentionally looked up via the module attribute (not imported as a name) so the seam test can monkey-patch the module-level handle_message. Verified by TestDispatcherDelegatesWithoutAck (2 tests: forwards msg+deps; never calls msg.ack), TestDispatcherDoesNotAckSeam (verbatim seam test from task brief), and TestMalformedDelegation (no short-circuit on bad payloads).

completion_promises.criterion_text: _run_serve rebinds _serve_daemon.dispatch_payload to the composed dispatcher before the consumer's first fetch; the receipt-only _default_dispatch is no longer reachable (Group A scenario 'receipt-only stub no longer reachable').
completion_promises.evidence: Modified src/forge/cli/serve.py to add a compose_dispatch_chain seam (mirroring the recovery_reconcile_on_boot / consumer_reconcile_on_boot seam pattern). _run_serve now awaits compose_dispatch_chain(client) between the reconciles and state.set_chain_ready(True). The bind_production_dispatch_chain factory builds a closure that calls build_pipeline_consumer_deps + make_handle_message_dispatcher + assigns _serve_daemon.dispatch_payload = dispatcher. After this assignment runs in production, the receipt-only _default_dispatch is no longer reachable. The default no-op preserves backward compatibility for tests (TestServeCmdSmoke etc.) — they keep passing without a SQLite pool wiring.

completion_promises.criterion_text: _serve_state.chain_ready is True after the rebind; the healthz probe reports healthy on the next read (assuming subscription live).
completion_promises.evidence: _run_serve calls compose_dispatch_chain(client) BEFORE state.set_chain_ready(True), so the rebind always precedes the chain_ready flip. SubscriptionState.is_healthy() returns live AND chain_ready, so once the daemon's pull subscription binds, healthz reports 200. The existing FW10-001 boot-order tests (test_cli_serve_skeleton.py::TestRunServeBootOrder) still pass, confirming the boot order invariant is preserved.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Code follows project conventions: 88-char line limits, type hints, snake_case for functions/variables, PascalCase for classes, frozen=True dataclasses where appropriate, comprehensive docstrings on every public symbol, from __future__ import annotations. All 2151 tests in tests/cli + tests/forge pass with the new modules. No bare except clauses; specific exceptions caught (sqlite3.Error, DuplicateBuildError, ValueError); error messages include context.

test_output_summary: tests/cli/test_serve_dispatcher.py: 6 passed. tests/cli/test_serve_deps.py: 19 passed. Smoke gate 3 (pytest tests/cli tests/forge -k 'serve or supervisor or deps'): 238 passed, 1913 deselected. Full suite (tests/cli + tests/forge): 2151 passed, 22 warnings.
implementation_notes: Composed PipelineConsumerDeps factory and dispatcher closure for FEAT-FORGE-010 Wave 3. (1) src/forge/cli/_serve_dispatcher.py — make_handle_message_dispatcher(deps) returns a thin async closure that delegates to pipeline_consumer.handle_message via module-attribute lookup so seam tests can monkey-patch handle_message. The closure never acks; that lifecycle is owned by the state machine's ack_callback per ADR-SP-013. (2) src/forge/cli/_serve_deps.py — build_pipeline_consumer_deps(client, forge_config, sqlite_pool, *, async_task_starter=None) composes the four PipelineConsumerDeps fields. is_duplicate_terminal reads SQLite for the (feature_id, correlation_id) status, returning True only for COMPLETE/FAILED/CANCELLED/SKIPPED rows (matches forge.lifecycle.state_machine.TERMINAL_STATES). dispatch_build calls record_pending_build then dispatch_autobuild_async with the three Wave-2 protocol collaborators + the FW10-006 emitter; raises RuntimeError if async_task_starter is missing (TASK-FW10-008's wiring). publish_build_failed wraps PipelinePublisher.publish_build_failed and discards the redundant feature_id arg after consistency-checking it against payload.feature_id. (3) src/forge/cli/serve.py — added compose_dispatch_chain seam (default no-op) and bind_production_dispatch_chain factory. _run_serve awaits the seam between reconciles and chain_ready=True so the rebind happens before the consumer's first fetch. The seam pattern mirrors the recovery_reconcile_on_boot / consumer_reconcile_on_boot pattern that already exists in serve.py, keeping the existing FW10-001 boot-order tests green.
concerns: AsyncTaskStarter wiring is deferred to TASK-FW10-008 (Supervisor + AsyncSubAgentMiddleware). The deps factory accepts it as a kwarg and dispatch_build raises RuntimeError when invoked without it — this surfaces missing wiring loudly during integration rather than silently dropping builds. The default compose_dispatch_chain seam is a no-op, so the receipt-only _default_dispatch stub is still reachable until production wiring rebinds the seam. This is by design — the rebind happens via bind_production_dispatch_chain when the full deps graph (including SQLite pool, ForgeConfig, async_task_starter) is available. The next task in the chain (FW10-008 or operations wiring) is responsible for binding the seam in production.
requirements_addressed: AC-001 — build_pipeline_consumer_deps wires all four PipelineConsumerDeps fields
AC-002 — make_handle_message_dispatcher returns an async _MsgLike closure
AC-003 — closure delegates to handle_message and does not ack
AC-004 — _run_serve rebinds dispatch_payload via the new compose_dispatch_chain seam
AC-005 — chain_ready flips True after the rebind seam
AC-006 — code follows project lint/format conventions; all 2151 tests pass
